name: react-patterns
description: Modern React patterns, hooks, component architecture, and best practices

entries.title: Compound Components Pattern
entries.content: Use compound components for flexible, composable UI. Parent manages state via Context, children consume it. Ideal for Tabs, Accordions, Select dropdowns.

entries.title: Essential Custom Hooks Collection
entries.content: Reusable hooks: useLocalStorage (persistent state), useDebounce (delay input), useFetch (data fetching with loading/error), useMediaQuery (responsive breakpoints), useOnClickOutside (close modals), useIntersectionObserver (lazy loading).

entries.title: Error Boundary with Fallback UI
entries.content: Class-based error boundary that catches render errors. Provide a fallback UI, error logging, and retry mechanism. Use react-error-boundary package for functional approach.

entries.title: Render Props & Children as Function
entries.content: Share cross-cutting logic via render props. Component accepts a function as children or render prop, passes state/handlers to it. Useful for mouse tracking, form state, toggles.

entries.title: Context + useReducer for State Management
entries.content: Combine Context with useReducer for Redux-like state management without external libraries. Define actions, reducer, provider. Separate dispatch context for performance.

entries.title: Code Splitting with Suspense & lazy
entries.content: Use React.lazy() for route-level code splitting. Wrap with Suspense for loading fallback. Combine with React Router for per-route bundles.

entries.title: Form Handling with Validation
entries.content: Controlled form with field-level validation, error display, and submit handling. Use useForm hook pattern or react-hook-form for complex forms.

entries.title: Modal with Portal & Focus Trap
entries.content: Render modal outside DOM hierarchy using createPortal. Trap focus inside modal, close on Escape, prevent body scroll.
